chunk_summary: This section provides case-based approaches for stunting workup and counseling, addressing determinants like dietary diversity, recurrent diarrhea, and malabsorption, with tailored interventions for under-five, red-flag cases, and adolescents.
learning_objectives: Understand the classification and management of stunting without wasting in under-five children.
Identify red flags for intestinal disorders and initiate appropriate diagnostic tests and interventions.
Counsel adolescents with stunting and delayed puberty on nutrition, anemia management, and endocrine evaluation thresholds.
key_facts: HAZ score of -2.6 indicates stunting without wasting in Case 1.
Zinc supplementation and ORS are recommended for recurrent diarrhea management.
Coeliac serology (tTG-IgA and total IgA) is essential for diagnosing malabsorption in Case 2.
Adolescent stunting with delayed menarche requires growth velocity monitoring and endocrine referral if puberty does not progress within 6 months.
WASH interventions include safe water storage, soap availability, and clean play spaces.
reference_quote: ‘Short stature in childhood: challenges and choices’ emphasizes the importance of accurate length measurement and determinant-based interventions for stunting management.
chunk_heading: STUNTING, CHRONIC UNDERNUTRITION AND DETERMINANTS;APPLIED CASE VIGNETTES FOR STUNTING WORKUP AND COUNSELING;
